description.strikePrice: The price at which the holder of an option can buy (in the case of a call option) or sell (in the case of a put option) the underlying security when the option is exercised.
description.moneyness: Moneyness is the correlation between the strike and the underlying price, a positive value means the strike is in-the-money, whereas a negative value is out-the-money.
description.midpoint: The midpoint between the bid and ask price for the options contract.
description.priceChange: The difference between the current price and the previous day's settlement price.
description.percentChange: The difference between the current price and the previous day's settlement price expressed as a percentage.
description.volume: The total number of shares or contracts traded for the day.
description.openInterest: The total number of open contracts for the previous day that have been traded but not yet liquidated via offsetting trades.
description.openInterestChange: The change in the number of option contracts traded but not yet liquidated.
description.volatility: Implied Volatility is the estimated volatility of the option strike over the period of the option.
description.delta: Measures the sensitivity of an option's theoretical value to a change in the price of the underlying asset.
description.gamma: Measures the rate of change in the delta for each one-point increase in the underlying asset.
description.vega: Measures the sensitivity of the price of an option to changes in volatility.
description.theta: A measure of the time decay of an option, the dollar amount that an option will lose each day due to the passage of time.
description.optionType: Put or Call. A Call option gives you the right to buy a stock and a Put option gives you the right to sell a stock.
description.daysToExpiration: The number of days until the option expires.
description.averageVolatility: The average implied volatility of the calls and puts immediately above and below the underlying price.
description.historicVolatility30d: 30-Day Historical Volatility: The average deviation from the average price over the last 30 days.
description.baseNextEarningsDate: The next reported earnings date for the underlying asset, or the latest earnings date as reported by the company (if no future date has been released).
description.dividendExDate: The next ex-dividend date, or the latest ex-dividend date if no future date has been released.
description.baseTimeCode: The date on which the earnings report for the underlying asset is released.
description.expirationType: The type of option expiration: monthly or weekly.
description.impliedVolatilityRank1y: The ATM average implied volatility relative to the highest and lowest values over the past 1-year. If IV Rank is 100% this means the IV is at its highest level over the past 1-year.
description.symbolType: A Call option gives you the right to buy a stock and a Put option gives you the right to sell a stock.
